Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Janet Hamill archive
Hamill, Janet.
Smith, Patti.
8062
12 cubic feet. 12 cubic feet.
1967-2010. Correspondence, photographs, publications The Janet Hamill Archive contains extensive materials that document the life and work of Janet Hamill and illuminates Hamill's unique relationship with her longtime friend Patti Smith. Collection material in English 7590056
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

Date completed:

EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, March 2013
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Poet, performer and artist.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The Janet Hamill Archive contains extensive materials that document the life and work of Janet Hamill. The archive additionally illuminates Hamill's unique relationship with her longtime friend, poet, writer, singer-songwriter and artist Patti Smith. Included are correspondence (approximately 380 letters), manuscripts, journals, notebooks, diaries (several thousand pages dated 1967-2007) and photographs documenting Hamill's work. Also included are materials pertaining to Patti Smith consisting of correspondence (approximately 100 letters), manuscripts and notes concerning the book Just Kids (approximately 1,000 pages) and photographs of and by Patti Smith.
SERIES LIST
Series I: Correspondence
Subseries Ia: General Correspondence Subseries Ib: Publishing Correspondence Subseries Ic: Miscellaneous Correspondence
Series II: Writings by Janet Hamill
Series III: Announcements for Performances/Readings and Related Material
Series IV: Photographs
Series V: Audio and Visual Items
Series VI: Journals, Diaries and Notebooks
Series VII: Moving Star
Series VIII: Personal Effects and Miscellaneous Items
Series IX: Artwork by Others
Series X: Patti Smith
Subseries Xa: Correspondence with Janet Hamill Subseries Xb: Photographs of Patti Smith Subseries Xc: Artwork by Patti Smith Subseries Xd: Just Kids Manuscripts and Notes Subseries Xe: Assorted Materials Subseries Xf: Robert Mapplethorpe Subseries Xg: Performances and Events with Patti Smith and Janet Hamill Subseries Xh: Audio Materials Subseries Xi: Posters and Broadsides
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Fleurs du Mal: in Pattern and Prose, by Charles Baudelaire
The Letters of Charles Baudelaire to his Mother, 1833-1866, by Charles Baudelaire
Genioy Figura de Jorge Luis Borges , by Alicia Jurado

A 4 7/8 x 7 ½ inch wood (cigar?) box that PS created after visiting Federico García Lorca's niece and handling an "original" copy of Poeta en Nueva York. PS presented the box to JH. It contains items that she brought back from Spain and includes: linen gloves that PS wore while handling the García Lorca manuscript, an unburned votive candle; a dried flower; a picture of García Lorca; a paper lace doily; a British pound coin; a Spanish postcard from 1903 of an unidentified woman; and a slip of paper with "Octubre 2007" printed on it."Lorca Box"20078Subseries Xd: Just Kids Manuscripts and NotesEarly handwritten notes199491

Scope and Contents

JH performed with John Giorno and also discussed her work with Smith. Scope and Contents
A 4 7/8 x 7 ½ inch wood (cigar?) box that PS created after visiting Federico García Lorca's niece and handling an "original" copy of Poeta en Nueva York. PS presented the box to JH. It contains items that she brought back from Spain and includes: linen gloves that PS wore while handling the García Lorca manuscript, an unburned votive candle; a dried flower; a picture of García Lorca; a paper lace doily; a British pound coin; a Spanish postcard from 1903 of an unidentified woman; and a slip of paper with "Octubre 2007" printed on it."Lorca Box"20078Subseries Xd: Just Kids Manuscripts and NotesEarly handwritten notes199491
Scope and Contents
Inserted in back of pad is handwritten manuscript on notebook graph paper of the poem "Wild Leaves" that appears only in the paperback edition of Just Kids. Also inserted in back are a color photocopy of six SX-70 photographs of Jesse Smith (?) in a graveyard with Smith family gravestones and a photocopy from parts of a Los Angeles Times article about Robert Mapplethorpe's artwork.Handwritten notes on graph paperDate unknown92

Scope and Contents
Drawing with colored pencil of PS with JH, 1966. This was made while JH was living with PS's family. It is inscribed by PS: "Chaps, ltd. 1966. With love to Chaps." According to JH, this drawing had been in the possession of Robert Mapplethorpe for over twenty years. It was given back to PS, by his estate, after his death. Scope and Contents
JH and Patti Smith were brought to Boston by their old friend Patti Hudson. This was Smith's first Boston appearance in 16 years. They did an evening of readings together and had an exhibition. Smith showed Polaroids at the Art Market Gallery, and JH showed her watercolors. Included are: publicity around the events, some correspondence and a t-shirt made for the event.Liss Ard Festival, Co. Cork, IrelandSeptember 1997935-40
Scope and Contents
Patti Smith was the festival's first director in 1997. JH participated in the festival and did several readings, including a reading with Patti Smith and Nick Cave. Scope and Contents
The 2005 Meltdown was directed by Patti Smith. She performed along with many others, including JH, Antony and the Johnsons, Beth Orton, Billy Bragg, Cat Power, Joanna Newsom, John Cale, Lenny Kaye, Martha Wainwright, Marc Almond, Richard Hell, Roy Harper, Sinéad O'Connor, Television, and Yoko Ono. JH performed with John Giorno and also discussed her work with Smith. Included are: the event's program, stage pass, correspondence, letter of agreement, publicity, and 2 handwritten manuscripts in JH's hand.Summerstage2005105
